env.world: Deterministic triangular-array collapsed cohort-by-time Poisson-projection world: cohorts g in C encoded as WithTop (Fin T) (never-treated = top), calendar periods t in Fin T, cohort-time cells carry positive masses q_gt / q_gtN, untreated cohort means B_gt = bar_b_g*exp(gamma_t0), observed cohort means m_gt(delta) = B_gt*exp(D_gt*delta_gt), collapsed regressor r_gt in R^(|C|+T), and the Poisson pseudo-criteria L_Nc / L. This is the ambient world of every headline statement (all six operate on the limiting/finite-array collapsed quantities).
env.notes: bypass-justified: Causalean has NO Poisson/PPML world (grep 'Poisson|PPML|expMean|multiplicative' over Causalean/ returns only unrelated concentration/minimax/ML modules; grep over Causalean/Panel/ returns 0). The nonlinear pseudo-likelihood criteria L_Nc/L, their unique maximizers, strict concavity/coercivity, and the fitted means mu_star are all bespoke finite-dimensional convex analysis built over Mathlib (Fin, WithTop, Real.exp, StrictConcaveOn, IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn). REUSED building blocks: the cohort/never-treated encoding is Causalean.Panel.AdoptionPath (WithTop (Fin T), le/lt/isNeverTreated, verified present); the mean-weighted-FWL residual layer (see def:weighted-fwl-residual) reuses Causalean.Panel.Weighted.WeightedSupport.{tildeX,Q_XX,thetaHat,RankCondition} (Subspace/FWL), and thm:sharp additionally reuses WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs (ScalarFWL.lean:74) for the normal-equation FWL coefficient ratio in the IFT derivative step. No single Causalean structure realizes this world, so the world skeleton itself is define-local with those helpers imported.

env.world: Finite-N unit-and-time fixed-effect Poisson panel with potential outcomes: units i in Fin N with deterministic cohort labels G_i in C (n_gN units per cohort), unit baselines b_iN>0, unit-time regressor v_itN in R^(N+T) (intercept, unit-FE, time-FE, treatment D_it), unit-time observed mean m_itN(delta)=b_iN*exp(gamma_t0+D_it*delta_{G_i,t}), the unit-FE Poisson criterion L_N and its beta coordinate beta_Nstar, and the extended-MLE sample coefficient hat_beta_N. Potential outcomes Y_it(d) and the array sampling law Pcal_N with expectation E_Pcal_N enter ONLY through the two mean-defining assumptions.
env.notes: bypass-justified: honest_scope disclaims all sampling/inference content (no fixed-T consistency, influence function, variance, or coverage claim). The probability layer is therefore not needed at theorem level: the two PO assumptions (ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean, ass:proportional-effects) pin E_Pcal_N[Y_it(d)] to the deterministic mean m_itN(delta), and every downstream statement consumes m_itN, not the law. E_Pcal_N is treated as an abstract linear expectation functional / the means as primitives over Mathlib (no MeasureTheory import required). REUSED: unit-time assignment/history from Causalean.Panel.PO.TreatmentPath and cohort encoding from Causalean.Panel.AdoptionPath. Causalean's PO probability spaces are at a heavier (measure-theoretic) abstraction than this purely-algebraic mean-level statement, so binding is define-local.

ass:cohort-share-limit.notes: Modeling/sampling assumption on the deterministic triangular array: for every N>=|C| and g, n_gN>0 and n_gN/N -> pi_g in (0,1). Named Prop def (Filter.Tendsto of pi_gN to pi_g plus positivity). NOT a substrate fact Causalean proves (no Poisson/array-limit decl; grep 'cohort.*share|Tendsto.*share' in Causalean/Panel = not found). Kept as threaded hypothesis.

ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean.notes: Causal modeling assumption (multiplicative parallel trends): E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)] = b_iN*exp(gamma_t0), gamma_10=0. Named Prop def over the abstract mean functional. No exact Causalean discharge (grep 'parallel.*trend|exponential.*mean|multiplicative' Causalean/Panel = only unrelated hits). Threaded hypothesis.

ass:within-cohort-baseline-limit.notes: Novel deterministic-regularity sampling assumption: bar_b_gN = n_gN^{-1} sum_{i in I_gN} b_iN -> bar_b_g in (0,infinity). Named Prop def (Tendsto). Author-atomized modeling boundary; no Causalean analogue (not found). Threaded hypothesis, not a proof obligation.

ass:proportional-effects.notes: Causal modeling assumption: E_Pcal_N[Y_it(1)] = E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)]*exp(delta_{G_i,t}) on treated (i,t). Named Prop def. No Causalean discharge (grep 'proportional.*effect' Causalean = not found). Threaded hypothesis.

ass:collapsed-design-rank.notes: Regularity modeling assumption: sum_{g,t} q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite. Named Prop def (Matrix.PosDef of the Gram, or equivalently spanning of {r_gt}). Related in spirit to Causalean.Panel.Weighted.WeightedSupport.RankCondition (verified in FWL.lean:96) but that is nonsingularity of a residualized Gram Q_XX at a fixed weight/design, NOT the population r-Gram positive-definiteness used here through the effect-dependent weights -> PARTIAL fit, so kept as a threaded hypothesis rather than discharged. F3 may derive the WeightedSupport.RankCondition it needs from this atom internally.

ass:multicohort-frontier-scope.notes: Novel scope assumption: {2,3,4,infinity} subset C (three adoption waves + never-treated). Named Prop def (finite subset containment on WithTop (Fin T)). Pure scope restriction, no library analogue.

ass:strict-positive-effects.notes: Novel scope assumption: delta_gt>0 for every (g,t) with D_gt=1. Named Prop def. Defines the sign-reversal region, not an estimation class; no library analogue.

def:unit-fe-population-projection.notes: Construction: beta_Nstar(delta) = last coordinate of argmax_{theta_N in R^(N+T)} L_N(theta_N;delta). Build-inline over Mathlib (define L_N as a finite double sum with Real.exp; the argmax's beta coordinate). No Causalean Poisson-criterion decl exists (verified). The existence/uniqueness backing this def is discharged in lem:unit-fe-collapse.

def:collapsed-population-projection.notes: Construction bundle: L(theta;delta) (finite sum over C x Fin T of q_gt[m_gt r_gt'theta - exp(r_gt'theta)]), theta_star(delta)=argmax L, beta_star(delta)=last coord, mu_star_gt(delta)=exp(r_gt'theta_star). Build-inline over Mathlib. Central shared def used by all statements. Uniqueness backed by lem:pseudo-true-ppml-projection.

def:sample-unit-fe-ppml.notes: Construction: extended-MLE convention (unit effects in R union {-infinity}, exp(-infinity)=0, period-1 time normalization, smallest-Euclidean-norm finite (time,beta) selection; hat_beta_N=0 if no finite-beta maximizer). Build-inline over Mathlib (WithBot/EReal coordinates + finite argmax). Only binds the symbol hat_beta_N; appears in NO proved statement's hyps (honest_scope: no sampling/inference theorem), so it is a definitional stub with no downstream proof obligation.

def:weighted-fwl-residual.notes: Construction: rho_star(delta)=argmin_rho sum q_gt mu_star_gt [D_gt - X_gt'rho]^2 and Wtilde_gt(delta)=D_gt - X_gt'rho_star. This is EXACTLY a weighted-least-squares residualization, so reuse Causalean.Panel.Weighted.WeightedSupport: Wtilde = c.tildeX H D with c the WeightedSupport over the cohort-time index carrying weights proportional to q_gt*mu_star_gt(delta) and H the span of X_gt (verified: tildeX at Subspace.lean:185, Q_XX/thetaHat/fwl_identity/RankCondition at FWL.lean:67/91/161/96). BRIDGE to build (partial fit): WeightedSupport requires weights normalized to sum 1, whereas q_gt*mu_star_gt need not; F3 normalizes by their total (projection is scale-invariant in the weights). This is the substantive reuse of the Causalean linear-FWL substrate at the definition layer; the Schur-complement / last-row-of-H-inverse ratio in thm:sharp is proved via WeightedSupport.tildeX + scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs (which delivers the residualized coefficient ratio directly from the weighted normal equations), not the joint-minimizer rewrite fwl_identity.

def:global-sign-reversal-region.notes: Construction: R_T as a Prop/Set over tuples ((pi_g),(B_gt),(delta_gt)) collecting {2,3,4,inf} subset C, B_gt=bar_b_g exp(gamma_t0), delta_gt>0 on treated, collapsed Gram PD, and beta_star(delta)<0. Build-inline over Mathlib (Set/Prop of a Sigma of finite data). No library analogue.

def:four-cohort-witness.notes: Construction: explicit W4 config (T=4, C={2,3,4,inf}, equal cohort counts m, b_iN=1, gamma_t0=0, delta_gt=log(101/100) except delta_{2,4}=log 4). Build-inline over Mathlib as concrete numeric data. Regression-test fixture for prop:four-cohort-sign-reversal.

def:frontier-elimination-handle.notes: Construction: the elimination handle producing Phi = M*A - sum_{g,t} D_gt R_g C_t from h_gt=pi_g B_gt exp(D_gt delta_gt) with row sums R_g, column sums C_t, total M, treated total A (the beta=0 conditional margin fit mu^0_gt = R_g C_t/(pi_g M) and treatment score s_0 = Phi/(TM)). Build-inline over Mathlib (finite sums, explicit algebra). Consumed by thm:primitive-global-frontier.

lem:unit-fe-collapse.notes: Crux, build-inline. Unique beta_Nstar exists; equals beta coord of the unique maximizer of L_Nc; beta_Nstar -> beta_star. Heaviest analytic piece: (i) finite-N unit-to-cohort profiling identity L_N = L_Nc + K_N via the within-cohort baseline factorization; (ii) strict concavity + coercivity of L_N and L_Nc (Mathlib StrictConcaveOn / negative-definite Hessian, IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn); (iii) argmax convergence under compact-uniform convergence L_Nc -> L (subsequence + boundedness argument from proof_tex). No open math and no CAD; all standard finite-dimensional convex analysis in Mathlib. Search: grep 'argmax|Tendsto.*max|epiconverg' Causalean = no reusable stability lemma, so built from Mathlib primitives.

lem:pseudo-true-ppml-projection.notes: Crux, build-inline. theta_star unique and solves the score sum_{g,t} q_gt r_gt[m_gt - mu_star_gt]=0. Standard: gradient/Hessian of L, strict concavity (negative-definite Hessian under the rank atom), coercivity, interior first-order condition. Small-to-medium Mathlib convex-analysis proof; no library gap.

thm:sharp-ppml-forbidden-sign.notes: Crux, build-inline. The headline derivative identity d beta_star/d delta_ks = q_ks B_ks exp(delta_ks) Wtilde_ks / (sum q_gt mu_star_gt Wtilde_gt^2), sign rule = sign(Wtilde_ks). Technique: implicit function theorem (Mathlib HasStrictFDerivAt / implicit-function API) applied to F(theta,delta)=score, giving the differentiated score in the pseudo-true weighted-FWL geometry with Hessian H=sum q mu r r' positive definite. The Schur/FWL derivative step reuses Causalean.Panel.Weighted.WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs (ScalarFWL.lean:74, verified): it turns EXACTLY the two weighted score/normal equations (h_normal_X on the treatment regressor D and h_normal_H on the nuisance span H=span{X_gt}) plus positive residual energy hpos (0 < ip tildeX tildeX = sum q mu Wtilde^2 = Schur complement S>0) into the residualized ratio beta = ip(tildeX,Y)/ip(tildeX,tildeX), which is precisely the last-coordinate-of-H^{-1} ratio Wtilde_ks/S needed here. This replaces the earlier fwl_identity citation, which only rewrites a joint WLS minimizer into thetaHat and does NOT supply the normal-equation FWL ratio for the IFT derivative. The residualized regressor tildeX and weighted inner product ip are the same WeightedSupport layer already reused by def:weighted-fwl-residual. The IFT differentiation, the Schur/positive-definite Hessian setup, and all PPML-specific mean/score algebra remain define-local over Mathlib. Medium-hard but standard; no open content.

prop:four-cohort-sign-reversal.notes: Crux, build-inline. W4 in R_4: all treated effects positive, (2,4) largest (log 4 > log(101/100)), beta_star<0 via Phi=-11559/32000<0; explicit Wtilde table = [[-3,3,1,-1],...]/8 at the no-effect point, Wtilde_{2,4}=-1/8, d beta_star/d delta_{2,4}=-1/10<0, then continuity gives an open positive-effect neighborhood (uses thm:sharp IFT continuity). Mostly explicit numeric algebra (norm_num/ring/decide over Fin 4 x Fin 4) plus a continuity/open-ball argument. No library gap. SYNC-BACK F2: removed the two assumption hypotheses because W4 is a concrete construction and the theorem must derive its scope and strict positivity internally; threading either property would assume a structural part of the conclusion.

prop:homogeneous-effect-reduction.notes: Crux, build-inline. Homogeneous reduction: delta_gt=delta_0 => beta_star=delta_0 and mu_star_gt=B_gt exp(D_gt delta_0). Short proof: log m_gt lies in the collapsed design, cellwise maximizer of f_m(eta)=m eta - exp eta at eta=log m, uniqueness by the rank atom. Small Mathlib convex-analysis proof.

thm:primitive-global-frontier.notes: Crux, build-inline. sign(beta_star)=sign(Phi) via the beta=0 conditional margin fit mu^0 = R_g C_t/(pi_g M) (matches all nuisance margins) and strict-monotone-gradient argument (0 > (theta_star-theta^0)'[grad L(theta_star)-grad L(theta^0)] = -beta_star s_0, s_0=Phi/(TM)); zero-level set = {Phi=0}; R_T = {Phi<0} under the scope/rank/positivity atoms; Part II explicit T=4 threshold Phi=(5x^2+12x-2y-15)/16 with cutoff 4441/4000 (norm_num); Part III diagnose-then-PTT identities (Bobs_gt=B_gt, tau_gt=exp(delta_gt)-1, PTT>0). All finite algebra + the shared strict-concavity gradient lemma. No open math.

file_layout.contents: S1/S2 world skeletons + all 7 assumption Prop defs + def:collapsed-population-projection, def:unit-fe-population-projection, def:sample-unit-fe-ppml
def:weighted-fwl-residual + Schur/FWL bridge lemmas over Causalean.Panel.Weighted.WeightedSupport
def:global-sign-reversal-region, def:four-cohort-witness, def:frontier-elimination-handle + shared Phi algebra
thin barrel importing the topic-split helper modules
